# IGDB Submission Profile: Tailuge Billiards

- **Game Title:** Tailuge Billiards
- **Primary Genre:** Sports
- **Themes / Sub-Genres:** Simulator / Realistic
- **Target Platforms:** Web Browser, Linux, PC (Windows)
- **Game Modes:** Single Player, Local Multiplayer, Online Multiplayer

## Summary / Description
Tailuge Billiards is a free, lightweight, high-fidelity cue sports simulator designed to bring professional-grade physics to any modern web browser. Developed with a focus on technical accuracy and performance, the simulator runs seamlessly on everything from high-end PCs to low-power devices like the Raspberry Pi 4 using a high-performance WebGL-based rendering engine.

At the core of Tailuge Billiards is a sophisticated physics engine that transcends basic arcade mechanics. It implements rigorous mathematical models for ball-to-ball elastic collisions, incorporating the "throw effect" and friction-based tangential impulses. The simulation accounts for complex ball-surface interactions, including sliding and rolling transitions, as well as advanced spin mechanics such as backspin, topspin, sidespin, and masse.

Cushion interactions are modeled using the Mathavan and Stronge compliant cushion theories, ensuring realistic deflection and energy dissipation across various slip regimes. Whether playing Nine-ball, Eight-ball, Snooker, or the demanding Three-cushion billiards, players will find a simulator that respects the nuances of the game.

Tailuge Billiards can be launched instantly in any browser without installation, or configured as a standalone desktop or mobile launcher. It features a robust multiplayer lobby for online challenges, replay recording/playback, and integrated tools for shot analysis and physics debugging.
